description: Apply the jackknife leave-one-out resampling technique — systematically excluding one observation at a time to estimate the bias and standard error of a statistic without parametric assumptions

fields.name: statistic
fields.label: mean | median | variance | std_dev | correlation | custom

core_mechanics.process: Construct n leave-one-out subsamples by omitting each observation in turn; compute the statistic on each
core_mechanics.without_replacement: Each jackknife subsample has size n-1 with one unique observation removed (NOT random resampling)
core_mechanics.deterministic: Unlike bootstrap, jackknife produces the same result every run for a given sample

key_formulas.jackknife_replicates: theta_hat_(i) = statistic applied to sample with observation i removed

sampling.bootstrap: Random sampling WITH replacement; produces B random resamples
sampling.jackknife: Deterministic leave-one-out; produces exactly n subsamples

coverage.jackknife: Visits only n of the 2^n possible subsets

purpose.jackknife: Simple bias and variance estimation; computationally cheap

strengths.bias_reduction: Directly targets and removes the leading-order bias of the estimator
strengths.computational_efficiency: Only n evaluations required vs thousands for bootstrap
strengths.deterministic_reproducibility: Identical output each run; no random seeds

limitations.statistic_smoothness: Fails for non-smooth statistics such as the median or quantiles
limitations.less_accurate_se: Typically less accurate standard errors than bootstrap
limitations.assumes_small_perturbation: Leave-one-out approximates infinitesimal perturbation; breaks down for influential observations

applications.sharpe_ratio_uncertainty: Jackknife SE of Sharpe ratio using historical returns
applications.credit_model_validation: Leave-one-firm-out stability testing of scoring models
applications.outlier_diagnosis: Large theta_hat_(i) - theta_hat_full indicates influential observation i

validation.sample_size: n >= 3 (need at least one observation to remove and still estimate)
validation.statistic_smoothness: Warn if statistic is known to be non-smooth (e.g., median)

run_jackknife.description: Execute n leave-one-out replicates and aggregate

compute_bias_correction.description: Apply jackknife bias correction to the full-sample statistic

non_smooth_statistic.description: Statistic is known to be non-smooth; jackknife unreliable

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply the jackknife to non-smooth statistics such as the median
anti_patterns.why: Leave-one-out changes the median only at discrete jumps; jackknife SE formula assumes smoothness and gives biased estimates — use bootstrap instead

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use the jackknife to build confidence intervals for heavily skewed statistics
anti_patterns.why: Jackknife assumes near-symmetric sampling distribution; for skewed statistics (e.g., variance, ratios) BCa bootstrap CIs are preferred

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore the bias-correction factor (n-1) when computing jackknife bias
anti_patterns.why: The (n-1) multiplier is essential to jackknife theory; omitting it produces a biased bias-estimate (ironically)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret large leave-one-out differences as statistical noise
anti_patterns.why: A single observation that moves theta substantially is a high-leverage point; document and investigate rather than averaging away

bootstrap_comparison.cost_per_run: n evaluations vs B >= 1000 for bootstrap
bootstrap_comparison.use_when: Sample size moderate; statistic is smooth; speed matters
bootstrap_comparison.avoid_when: Quantile-based statistics or very small n
